perturbation.original_text: Sponsor shall pay $*** to SDBF pursuant to the following: $ *** due on or before April 30, 2008 $ *** due on or before June 30, 2008 $ *** due on or before August 31, 2008
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor shall remit $*** to SDBF pursuant to the following: $ *** due on or before April 30, 2008 $ *** due on or before June 30, 2008 $ *** due on or before August 31, 2008
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'pay' to 'remit' introduces ambiguity as 'remit' is often associated with sending money without implying the same contractual obligation as 'pay'. While seemingly similar, 'pay' is a legally binding term implying a debt settlement, 'remit' just implies the transfer of money, but does not confirm the legal aspect of sponsorship obligation. Later clauses rely on the term 'payment' creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Sponsorship Benefits to be furnished by SDBF to Sponsor hereunder, Sponsor shall pay to SDBF the Annual Payments set forth in the Agreement Summary on the dates set forth therein. Any payment due pursuant to this Section that is not received by SDBF within fifteen (15) days after such payment is due shall bear interest from the date the payment was due until paid, at a per annum rate of interest equal to the prime rate of interest as published in the Wall Street Journal, plus five (5) percentage points, or if such interest rate is not permitted by applicable law, at the highest non-usurious interest rate permitted by applicable law. At its sole option, SDBF may terminate this Agreement in the event that a payment required hereunder is not made within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due and may exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'pay' to 'remit' introduces ambiguity regarding the contractual obligation. While 'pay' clearly signifies a debt settlement, 'remit' merely implies the transfer of money. This ambiguity contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which explicitly states 'Sponsor shall pay to SDBF the Annual Payments,' emphasizing a legally binding obligation. The contradicted text also outlines penalties for late 'payment,' a term directly linked to the original, more legally binding, term 'pay.' Therefore, the change undermines the clarity of the payment obligation and the associated consequences, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the sponsorship obligation is not clearly tied to the term 'remit'.

perturbation.original_text: All use by Sponsor and Sponsor's invitees of any hospitality benefits granted hereunder shall be subject to the rules, regulations and policies established from time to time by SDBF and/or the Padres and may be revoked in the event of the failure of Sponsor or Sponsor's invitees to comply with such rules, regulations and policies.
perturbation.changed_text: All use by Sponsor and Sponsor's guests of any hospitality benefits granted hereunder shall be subject to the rules, regulations and policies established from time to time by SDBF and/or the Padres and may be revoked in the event of the failure of Sponsor or Sponsor's representatives to comply with such rules, regulations and policies.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'invitees' to 'guests' and 'Sponsor's invitees' to 'Sponsor's representatives' creates confusion about who is bound by the rules and regulations. The original text explicitly includes 'Sponsor's invitees,' establishing a clear line of responsibility. Now, the term guest is colloquial and the obligations, relies on the undefined concept of 'Sponsor's representatives', potentially excluding some individuals benefiting from the hospitality benefits. This creates an in-text contradiction as the guests are the ones attending but now there is a disconnect in who is liable if those guests don't follow the rules, it create a uncertainty in the event of non-compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: With respect to any hospitality benefits granted hereunder, Sponsor and Sponsor's invitees shall maintain proper decorum, comply with all laws, rules and regulations of all governmental authorities, not suffer or permit the continuation of any use or manner of use of the hospitality benefits in violation of any applicable Founders Club Agreements, not create any nuisance, and not take any action which either diminishes hazard insurance coverage for the Ballpark or increases the premium payable for such insurance. Sponsor and Sponsor's invitees shall be bound by and observe the terms and conditions upon which any admission tickets are issued or sold.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'invitees' to 'representatives' introduces a contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly states obligations for 'Sponsor and Sponsor's invitees.' By changing 'invitees' to 'representatives' in the modified text, it creates ambiguity as to whether the same obligations apply to the new group. The term 'guests' are the ones attending but now there is a disconnect in who is liable if those guests don't follow the rules. This creates uncertainty in the event of non-compliance, as the contradicted section relies on the explicit term 'invitees' to define who is bound by the rules, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If (i) Sponsor fails to pay any amounts within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due pursuant to this Agreement, (ii) Sponsor otherwise defaults in the performance or observance of Sponsor's duties and obligations under this Agreement and fails to cure such default within fifteen (15) days after notice thereof by SDBF, or (iii) Sponsor makes an assignment of substantially all of its assets for the benefit of creditors, is adjudicated bankrupt, files a voluntary petition in bankruptcy or a voluntary petition or an answer seeking reorganization, arrangement, readjustment of its debts or for any other relief under Title 11 of the United States Code or any successor or other federal or state insolvency law ("Bankruptcy Law"), has filed against it an involuntary petition in bankruptcy or seeking reorganization, arrangement, readjustment of its debts or for any other relief under any Bankruptcy Law, which petition is not discharged within thirty (30) days, or applies for or permits the appointment of a receiver or trustee for its assets, SDBF may, at its option, with or without notice or demand, (x) terminate the rights of Sponsor under this Agreement, whereupon SDBF shall have no further obligation of any kind to Sponsor, and Sponsor shall have no right to recovery or offset of any amounts previously paid to SDBF under this Agreement, and (y) exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF.
perturbation.changed_text: If (i) Sponsor fails to pay any amounts within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due pursuant to this Agreement, (ii) Sponsor otherwise defaults in the performance or observance of Sponsor's duties and obligations under this Agreement and fails to rectify such default within fifteen (15) days after notice thereof by SDBF, or (iii) Sponsor makes an assignment of substantially all of its assets for the benefit of creditors, is adjudicated bankrupt, files a voluntary petition in bankruptcy or a voluntary petition or an answer seeking reorganization, arrangement, readjustment of its debts or for any other relief under Title 11 of the United States Code or any successor or other federal or state insolvency law ("Bankruptcy Law"), has filed against it an involuntary petition in bankruptcy or seeking reorganization, arrangement, readjustment of its debts or for any other relief under any Bankruptcy Law, which petition is not discharged within thirty (30) days, or applies for or permits the appointment of a receiver or trustee for its assets, SDBF may, at its option, with or without notice or demand, (x) terminate the rights of Sponsor under this Agreement, whereupon SDBF shall have no further obligation of any kind to Sponsor, and Sponsor shall have no right to recovery or offset of any amounts previously paid to SDBF under this Agreement, and (y) exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'cure' to 'rectify' introduces uncertainty in the consequences of not fulfilling the terms in the agreement, as 'cure' in legal contracts has a well-defined scope that requires returning to a previous state that is acceptable and that is free from the error of the defect, by changing the term 'cure' by 'rectify' it creates a in-text contradiction to how to proceed with resolving the breach and creates a contradiction to what can be considered a successful resolution, also it may lead to confusion on wether all previous state, should return to their previous state.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Default. If (i) Sponsor fails to pay any amounts within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due pursuant to this Agreement, (ii) Sponsor otherwise defaults in the performance or observance of Sponsor's duties and obligations under this Agreement and fails to rectify such default within fifteen (15) days after notice thereof by SDBF, or (iii) Sponsor makes an assignment of substantially all of its assets for the benefit of creditors, is adjudicated bankrupt, files a voluntary petition in bankruptcy or a voluntary petition or an answer seeking reorganization, arrangement, readjustment of its debts or for any other relief under Title 11 of the United States Code or any successor or other federal or state insolvency law ("Bankruptcy Law"), has filed against it an involuntary petition in bankruptcy or seeking reorganization, arrangement, readjustment of its debts or for any other relief under any Bankruptcy Law, which petition is not discharged within thirty (30) days, or applies for or permits the appointment of a receiver or trustee for its assets, SDBF may, at its option, with or without notice or demand, (x) terminate the rights of Sponsor under this Agreement, whereupon SDBF shall have no further obligation of any kind to Sponsor, and Sponsor shall have no right to recovery or offset of any amounts previously paid to SDBF under this Agreement, and (y) exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF. Any notice required to be given hereunder shall be in lieu of, and not in addition to, any notice required under California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1161 or any similar or successor law.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the term 'cure' which has a specific legal meaning related to remedying a default and returning to a prior compliant state. The changed text replaces 'cure' with 'rectify,' which introduces ambiguity because 'rectify' lacks the same established legal definition and may not necessarily require a complete return to the prior compliant state. The contradicted text reiterates this altered term, which causes an in-text contradiction because the meaning of the remedy is now uncertain, making it difficult to determine what constitutes a successful resolution of a default.
